title: reinwardtia indica subsp. roxburghii
intro: Reinwardtia indica subsp. roxburghii, commonly known as Chhota Rush or Indian staff tree, is a plant species native to the Himalayan regions of India and China. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Reinwardtia indica subsp. roxburghii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatoid arthritis, and act as an antiseptic agent. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant's bark, roots, and leaves are used to prepare various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
